summary: Iteration 6 retest of the two iter5 bugs in routes/companion.py auto-memory pipeline. Both fixes verified. Full suite 67/67 passing (51 regression + 16 iter5). Targeted retest of TestCompanionMemoryTTL (2 tests) + TestJaccardDedupe (1 test) all PASS in 58s. Previously flaky TTL test now reliably passes because _prune_auto_memories runs unconditionally in a try/finally regardless of AI parse outcome. Jaccard dedupe now correctly suppresses near-duplicate auto memories about the same fact.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/companion.py: clean fix — _prune_auto_memories extracted as a standalone async helper, _auto_extract_memories wraps the AI/parse block in try/finally so prune ALWAYS runs. _tokens applies light stemming (strips trailing 's' on tokens >3 chars) which correctly merges years/year, loves/love. DEDUPE_THRESHOLD lowered to 0.55. Looks correct and minimal.

Minor (carry-over from iter5, not blocking): family_models.Holiday.todos still typed as List[dict] instead of typed sub-model; routes/family.py still missing PUT/PATCH for memories; routes/audio.py hard-coded limit 500. These are not regressions and not in scope for this retest.
seed_data_creation: TestCompanionMemoryTTL inserts up to 206 synthetic auto-memory docs into companion_memories collection; cleaned up via fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iter1-5 bugs resolved. Suite is green: pytest /app/backend/tests/ → 67 passed in ~3 min. Companion auto-memory pipeline is now well-behaved (TTL prune always runs, Jaccard dedupe with stemming at 0.55 catches reworded duplicates).
rca of the issue: Both iter5 bugs fixed correctly: (1) Jaccard — _tokens() now strips trailing 's' on tokens >3 chars (years→year, loves→love), threshold 0.55 instead of 0.7. Verified with TestJaccardDedupe message pair: msg1 vs msg2 about Aisha → only 0-1 net new auto memories (down from ~3 previously). (2) TTL prune — _prune_auto_memories extracted to a standalone async function; _auto_extract_memories now wraps AI+parse in try, calls prune in finally, so cap of 200 is enforced even when AI returns non-JSON. Verified with 205 seeded old auto memories + junk 'x'*200 message → count drops to <=200 within 25s; pinned memory survives.
